intro: Corneria novoguineensis, also known as New Guinea Corneria, is a plant species native to the island of New Guinea. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Corneria novoguineensis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory infections.
